matcher.coveragePreview: surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes middle school mathematics students cube and cuboid properties cylinder surface area and volume cone lateral vs total surface area sphere and hemisphere calculations surface areas and volumes: 3-d shapes introduction to session topic session: surface areas and volumes (part i) focus: cubes, cuboids, cylinders, cones, spheres what we will learn today learning objectives identify properties of cubes, cuboids, cylinders, cones, spheres, and hemispheres. recall and apply formulas for total surface area (tsa) and volume. distinguish between curved surface area (csa) and tsa where applicable. cubes and cuboids: formulas & logic cube properties cuboid properties [object object] cuboid: length(l), width(w), height(h). tsa cuboid = 2(lw + wh + hl) volume cuboid = l × w × h example: calculating surface area of a room application of cuboid formulas [object object] [object object] step 2: formula needed? lateral surface area (lsa) only.
